content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

May 20, 1997

Stanley E. Remelmeyer
Assistant City Attorney
City of Redondo Beach
415 Diamond Street
Post Office Box 270
Redondo Beach, California 90277-0270

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-97-217

Dear Mr. Remelmeyer:

This letter is a response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please bear in mind that nothing in this letter should be
construed as an evaluation of any conduct which may already have taken place. Further, this
letter is based on the facts surrounding your questions as you have presented them to us. The
Commission does not act as finder of fact in providing advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC
Ops. 71.)

QUESTION

May the City of Redondo Beach (the "City") award a contract to evaluate and fine tune
the staff proposed design of a bike path improvement to the consulting firm, John M. Cruikshank
Consultants ("JMC"), if John M. Cruikshank is a member of the City's Harbor Commission?

CONCLUSION

Nothing in the Act prohibits the award of the contract to JMC. We cannot determine
whether awarding of the contract is appropriate pursuant to other laws and mandates governing
the City.

_____________________________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, Sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

File No. I-97-217
Page 2

FACTS

On January 22, 1997, the City decided to hire a consulting firm to evaluate and fine tune
the staff proposed bike path improvements; the route of the bike path extends through the King
Harbor areas of the City. The City sent Requests for Proposals ("RFP"s") to five consulting
firms on March 4, 1997. The only response to the pre-bid jobwalk was JMC. Six additional
RFP's were sent out by the City on March 12, 1997. On April 9, 1997, the City's Engineering
Department received four responses to the RFP's; JMC was the lowest responsible bidder.

John Cruikshank ("Cruikshank") is the president of JMC. Cruikshank is also a member
of the City's Harbor Commission. The Harbor Commission is created pursuant to and governed
by Article 7, Chapter 9, Title 2 of the Redondo Beach Municipal Code. The powers and duties
of the Commission include, inter alia, holding hearings on any matter concerning the
commercial or recreational development of the harbor, furthering the commercial and
recreational development of the harbor and advising the City Council on any matter concerning
the Harbor when so requested by the Council. The Harbor Commission has jurisdiction over the
King Harbor areas of the City where portions of the bike path are located.

ANALYSIS

Nothing in the Act prohibits the award of the bike path contract to JMC. Please be
advised, however, that the Act and the FPPC only regulate, in this instance, the conflicts of
interest of public officials. Accordingly, other laws, regulations and practical impediments may
exist which would preclude the award of the contract to JMC; we cannot and do not provide any
advice with respect to anything other than the Act.

You have not asked us to advise as to the potential conflicts of interest involving
Cruikshank. Further, it does not appear that you have authorization to request this advice. For
your assistance, should this issue be raised later, I have included a copy of the Davidson Advice
Letter, No. I-95-111, which discusses a similar situation. We would encourage you to ask
Cruikshank to request written advice regarding his potential conflicts of interest under the Act.

File No. I-97-217
Page 3


If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Lisa L. Ditora
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:LLD:ak
